tool_input.content:
# Stepper Component Implementation Plan ## Context The shadcn/ui v4 component library lacks a Stepper component for multi-step wizards and onboarding flows. This is a common UI pattern that does not exist in the @base-ui/react primitives, so we build it from scratch using React context (following the same pattern as the Carousel component). We need to create the component in both the base registry location and the styled (base-nova) location, plus a demo example and registry entries. **Constraint:** Do NOT modify any existing component files. Only create new files and append registry entries. --- ## Files to Create (3 new files) | # | File | Purpose | |---|------|---------| | 1 | apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx | Base registry component (semantic cn- tokens) | | 2 | apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/stepper.tsx | Styled version (full Tailwind classes) | | 3 | apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx | Demo with multiple variations | ## Files to Modify (2 registry files -- append only) | # | File | Change | |---|------|--------| | 4 | apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/_registry.ts | Append stepper entry to ui array | | 5 | apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts | Append stepper-example entry to examples array | No changes needed to apps/v4/registry/bases/base/registry.ts -- it already spreads ...ui and ...examples. --- ## Step 1 -- Base Registry Component **File:** apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx Follows the Carousel pattern: custom React context, "use client", imports from @/registry/bases/base/lib/utils, uses cn- semantic tokens and IconPlaceholder. ### Two Contexts **StepperContext** (root-level, shared state): - activeStep: number - orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" - onStepChange: (step: number) => void - linear: boolean **StepperItemContext** (per-item, derived state): - step: number - isActive: boolean - isCompleted: boolean - isDisabled: boolean ### Sub-Components **Stepper** -- Root container + context provider - Props: React.ComponentProps<"div"> and defaultStep?: number, step?: number, onStepChange?: (step: number) => void, orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical", linear?: boolean - Controlled/uncontrolled pattern: defaultStep (default 0) or step+onStepChange - Renders div with data-slot="stepper" data-orientation and role="group" aria-label="progress" - Class: cn("cn-stepper group/stepper flex", orientation-based flex-col/flex-row gap-4, className) **StepperItem** -- Individual step wrapper + item context provider - Props: React.ComponentProps<"div"> and step: number, disabled?: boolean - Computes isActive, isCompleted from stepper context - Sets data-slot="stepper-item" and data-state="active" | "completed" | "inactive" - Horizontal: flex items-center gap-2, with flex-1 for non-last items - Vertical: flex flex-col gap-2 **StepperTrigger** -- Clickable circle indicator - Props: React.ComponentProps<"button"> - Renders button with data-slot="stepper-trigger" - Shows step number (step + 1) by default; shows checkmark icon when completed - Calls onStepChange(step) on click (respects linear constraint and disabled state) - aria-current="step" when active - Circle styling: inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-full size-8 shrink-0 border-2 text-sm font-medium transition-colors - Uses IconPlaceholder for the checkmark icon (base version) **StepperTitle** -- Step title text - Props: React.ComponentProps<"div"> - data-slot="stepper-title", class with cn-stepper-title cn-font-heading text-sm font-medium - Text color varies by state via parent data-state + group selectors **StepperDescription** -- Step description text - Props: React.ComponentProps<"div"> - data-slot="stepper-description", class with cn-stepper-description text-xs text-muted-foreground **StepperSeparator** -- Connector line between steps - Props: React.ComponentProps<"div"> - Placed inside a StepperItem, reads useStepperItem() for completed state - data-slot="stepper-separator", data-state="completed" | "inactive" - Horizontal: h-0.5 flex-1; Vertical: w-0.5 ml-4 min-h-4 - Color: cn-stepper-separator token; completed = primary, inactive = border **StepperContent** -- Content panel for active step - Props: React.ComponentProps<"div"> and step: number - Placed outside StepperItem, at Stepper root level; uses useStepper() to check if step === activeStep - Only renders children when active - data-slot="stepper-content" ### Exports Stepper, StepperItem, StepperTrigger, StepperTitle, StepperDescription, StepperSeparator, StepperContent, useStepper --- ## Step 2 -- Styled Version (base-nova) **File:** apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/stepper.tsx Same structure as Step 1 but: - Imports from @/lib/utils (not @/registry/bases/base/lib/utils) - Imports CheckIcon from lucide-react directly (not IconPlaceholder) - Full Tailwind utility classes instead of cn- semantic tokens: Key styling: - Trigger (active): border-primary bg-primary text-primary-foreground - Trigger (completed): border-primary bg-primary text-primary-foreground - Trigger (inactive): border-muted-foreground/25 bg-muted text-muted-foreground - Separator (completed): bg-primary - Separator (inactive): bg-border - Title (active/completed): text-foreground - Title (inactive): text-muted-foreground --- ## Step 3 -- Demo Example **File:** apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx Follows the pattern from tabs-example.tsx: "use client", default export, ExampleWrapper + individual Example sub-components. ### Variations 1. **StepperBasic** -- Horizontal 3-step with titles only 2. **StepperWithDescriptions** -- Horizontal with titles + descriptions 3. **StepperVertical** -- Vertical orientation 4. **StepperWithContent** -- Full wizard with content panels + Next/Previous buttons (uses React.useState) 5. **StepperLinear** -- linear=true, can only go forward 6. **StepperControlled** -- Controlled state with external Next/Previous/Reset buttons ### Imports - Example, ExampleWrapper from @/registry/bases/base/components/example - Button from @/registry/bases/base/ui/button - All Stepper sub-components from @/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper --- ## Step 4 -- Registry Entries ### 4a. Append to apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/_registry.ts Add before the closing ] of the ui array: a stepper entry with name "stepper", type "registry:ui", files pointing to "ui/stepper.tsx", and meta.links for docs and examples. ### 4b. Append to apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts Add before the closing ] of the examples array: a stepper-example entry with name "stepper-example", title "Stepper", type "registry:example", registryDependencies ["stepper", "button", "example"], files pointing to "examples/stepper-example.tsx". --- ## Component API Usage Horizontal wizard with content: ```
Account Set up your account
Profile
Complete
 Account form... Profile form... Done! ``` Vertical: Stepper orientation="vertical" defaultStep={1} Linear (forward-only): Stepper linear defaultStep={0} Controlled: Stepper step={currentStep} onStepChange={setCurrentStep} --- ## Key Design Decisions 1. **Numeric 0-indexed steps** (not string values like Tabs) -- steppers have inherent ordering; step < activeStep = completed 2. **Explicit step prop on StepperItem** -- simpler and more predictable than auto-indexing from render order 3. **StepperSeparator inside StepperItem** -- can read useStepperItem() context to determine completed state 4. **StepperContent outside StepperItem** -- takes its own step prop, reads useStepper(), renders at root level below the step indicators 5. **linear prop** -- when true, only completed steps and the next step are clickable 6. **Controlled/uncontrolled** -- defaultStep for uncontrolled, step+onStepChange for controlled ## Reference Files (patterns to follow) - apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/carousel.tsx -- React context pattern, cn- tokens, IconPlaceholder - apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/carousel.tsx -- Styled version, lucide icons, @/lib/utils - apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/card.tsx -- Layout compound pattern with cn- tokens - apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/card.tsx -- Styled layout compound - apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/tabs.tsx -- Orientation handling with data-orientation + group selectors - apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/tabs-example.tsx -- Example file structure --- ## Verification 1. **TypeScript:** Run pnpm tsc --noEmit in apps/v4/ to verify no type errors 2. **Registry parse:** The registry.ts runs z.array(registryItemSchema).parse(...) at import time -- if the entry is malformed it will throw 3. **Visual:** Run pnpm dev in apps/v4/ and navigate to the stepper example page to verify all 6 demo variations render correctly in both horizontal and vertical orientations 4. **Build:** Run pnpm build to ensure the build pipeline picks up the new registry entries

python3 -c "
import os
os.makedirs('.claude/plans', exist_ok=True)
content = '''# Stepper Component Implementation Plan

## Context

The shadcn/ui v4 component library lacks a Stepper component for multi-step wizards and onboarding flows. This is a common UI pattern that does not exist in the base-ui/react primitives, so we build it from scratch using React context (following the same pattern as the Carousel component). We need to create the component in both the base registry location and the styled (base-nova) location, plus a demo example and registry entries.

Constraint: Do NOT modify any existing component files. Only create new files and append registry entries.

---

## Files to Create (3 new files)

1. apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx - Base registry component (semantic cn- tokens)
2. apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/stepper.tsx - Styled version (full Tailwind classes)
3. apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx - Demo with multiple variations

## Files to Modify (2 registry files, append only)

4. apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/_registry.ts - Append stepper entry to ui array
5. apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts - Append stepper-example entry to examples array

No changes needed to apps/v4/registry/bases/base/registry.ts, it already spreads ...ui and ...examples.

---

## Step 1: Base Registry Component

File: apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx

Follows the Carousel pattern: custom React context, use client, imports from registry/bases/base/lib/utils, uses cn- semantic tokens and IconPlaceholder.

### Two Contexts

StepperContext (root-level, shared state):
- activeStep: number
- orientation: horizontal or vertical
- onStepChange: (step: number) => void
- linear: boolean

StepperItemContext (per-item, derived state):
- step: number
- isActive: boolean
- isCompleted: boolean
- isDisabled: boolean

### Sub-Components

Stepper: Root container + context provider
- Props: React.ComponentProps div plus defaultStep, step, onStepChange, orientation, linear
- Controlled/uncontrolled pattern: defaultStep (default 0) or step+onStepChange
- data-slot=stepper, data-orientation, role=group, aria-label=progress
- Class: cn-stepper group/stepper flex, orientation-based flex-col/flex-row gap-4

StepperItem: Individual step wrapper + item context provider
- Props: React.ComponentProps div plus step: number, disabled?: boolean
- Computes isActive, isCompleted from stepper context
- data-slot=stepper-item, data-state=active/completed/inactive
- Horizontal: flex items-center gap-2 with flex-1 for non-last items
- Vertical: flex flex-col gap-2

StepperTrigger: Clickable circle indicator
- Props: React.ComponentProps button
- data-slot=stepper-trigger
- Shows step number (step + 1) by default; shows checkmark icon when completed
- Calls onStepChange(step) on click (respects linear constraint and disabled state)
- aria-current=step when active
- Circle styling: inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-full size-8 shrink-0 border-2 text-sm font-medium transition-colors
- Uses IconPlaceholder for the checkmark icon (base version)

StepperTitle: Step title text
- Props: React.ComponentProps div
- data-slot=stepper-title, cn-stepper-title cn-font-heading text-sm font-medium
- Text color varies by state via parent data-state + group selectors

StepperDescription: Step description text
- Props: React.ComponentProps div
- data-slot=stepper-description, cn-stepper-description text-xs text-muted-foreground

StepperSeparator: Connector line between steps
- Props: React.ComponentProps div
- Placed inside a StepperItem, reads useStepperItem() for completed state
- data-slot=stepper-separator, data-state=completed/inactive
- Horizontal: h-0.5 flex-1; Vertical: w-0.5 ml-4 min-h-4
- Color: cn-stepper-separator token; completed=primary, inactive=border

StepperContent: Content panel for active step
- Props: React.ComponentProps div plus step: number
- Placed outside StepperItem at Stepper root level; uses useStepper() to check step === activeStep
- Only renders children when active
- data-slot=stepper-content

### Exports

Stepper, StepperItem, StepperTrigger, StepperTitle, StepperDescription, StepperSeparator, StepperContent, useStepper

---

## Step 2: Styled Version (base-nova)

File: apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/stepper.tsx

Same structure as Step 1 but:
- Imports from lib/utils (not registry/bases/base/lib/utils)
- Imports CheckIcon from lucide-react directly (not IconPlaceholder)
- Full Tailwind utility classes instead of cn- semantic tokens

Key styling:
- Trigger active: border-primary bg-primary text-primary-foreground
- Trigger completed: border-primary bg-primary text-primary-foreground
- Trigger inactive: border-muted-foreground/25 bg-muted text-muted-foreground
- Separator completed: bg-primary
- Separator inactive: bg-border
- Title active/completed: text-foreground
- Title inactive: text-muted-foreground

---

## Step 3: Demo Example

File: apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx

Follows the pattern from tabs-example.tsx: use client, default export, ExampleWrapper + individual Example sub-components.

### Variations

1. StepperBasic: Horizontal 3-step with titles only
2. StepperWithDescriptions: Horizontal with titles + descriptions
3. StepperVertical: Vertical orientation
4. StepperWithContent: Full wizard with content panels + Next/Previous buttons (uses React.useState)
5. StepperLinear: linear=true, can only go forward
6. StepperControlled: Controlled state with external Next/Previous/Reset buttons

### Imports

- Example, ExampleWrapper from registry/bases/base/components/example
- Button from registry/bases/base/ui/button
- All Stepper sub-components from registry/bases/base/ui/stepper

---

## Step 4: Registry Entries

### 4a. Append to apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/_registry.ts

Add stepper entry with name stepper, type registry:ui, files pointing to ui/stepper.tsx, and meta.links for docs and examples URLs.

### 4b. Append to apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts

Add stepper-example entry with name stepper-example, title Stepper, type registry:example, registryDependencies [stepper, button, example], files pointing to examples/stepper-example.tsx.

---

## Key Design Decisions

1. Numeric 0-indexed steps (not string values like Tabs): steppers have inherent ordering; step < activeStep = completed
2. Explicit step prop on StepperItem: simpler and more predictable than auto-indexing from render order
3. StepperSeparator inside StepperItem: can read useStepperItem() context to determine completed state
4. StepperContent outside StepperItem: takes its own step prop, reads useStepper(), renders at root level below the step indicators
5. linear prop: when true, only completed steps and the next step are clickable
6. Controlled/uncontrolled: defaultStep for uncontrolled, step+onStepChange for controlled

## Reference Files (patterns to follow)

- apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/carousel.tsx: React context pattern, cn- tokens, IconPlaceholder
- apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/carousel.tsx: Styled version, lucide icons, lib/utils
- apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/card.tsx: Layout compound pattern with cn- tokens
- apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/card.tsx: Styled layout compound
- apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/tabs.tsx: Orientation handling with data-orientation + group selectors
- apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/tabs-example.tsx: Example file structure

---

## Verification

1. TypeScript: Run pnpm tsc --noEmit in apps/v4/ to verify no type errors
2. Registry parse: The registry.ts runs z.array(registryItemSchema).parse() at import time, if the entry is malformed it will throw
3. Visual: Run pnpm dev in apps/v4/ and navigate to the stepper example page to verify all 6 demo variations render correctly in both horizontal and vertical orientations
4. Build: Run pnpm build to ensure the build pipeline picks up the new registry entries
'''
with open('.claude/plans/linked-imagining-crown.md', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
print('Plan file written successfully')

tool_input.content:
# Stepper Component Implementation Plan

## Context

The shadcn/ui v4 component library lacks a Stepper component for multi-step wizards and onboarding flows. This is a common UI pattern that does not exist in the base-ui/react primitives, so we build it from scratch using React context (following the same pattern as the Carousel component). We need to create the component in both the base registry location and the styled (base-nova) location, plus a demo example and registry entries.

Constraint: Do NOT modify any existing component files. Only create new files and append registry entries.

---

## Files to Create (3 new files)

1. apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx - Base registry component (semantic cn- tokens)
2. apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/stepper.tsx - Styled version (full Tailwind classes)
3. apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx - Demo with multiple variations

## Files to Modify (2 registry files, append only)

4. apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/_registry.ts - Append stepper entry to ui array
5. apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts - Append stepper-example entry to examples array

No changes needed to apps/v4/registry/bases/base/registry.ts, it already spreads ...ui and ...examples.

---

## Step 1: Base Registry Component

File: apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx

Follows the Carousel pattern: custom React context, use client, imports from @/registry/bases/base/lib/utils, uses cn- semantic tokens and IconPlaceholder.

### Two Contexts

StepperContext (root-level, shared state):
- activeStep: number
- orientation: horizontal or vertical
- onStepChange: (step: number) => void
- linear: boolean

StepperItemContext (per-item, derived state):
- step: number
- isActive: boolean
- isCompleted: boolean
- isDisabled: boolean

### Sub-Components

Stepper: Root container + context provider
- Props: React.ComponentProps div plus defaultStep, step, onStepChange, orientation, linear
- Controlled/uncontrolled pattern: defaultStep (default 0) or step+onStepChange
- data-slot=stepper, data-orientation, role=group, aria-label=progress
- Class: cn-stepper group/stepper flex, orientation-based flex-col/flex-row gap-4

StepperItem: Individual step wrapper + item context provider
- Props: React.ComponentProps div plus step: number, disabled?: boolean
- Computes isActive, isCompleted from stepper context
- data-slot=stepper-item, data-state=active/completed/inactive
- Horizontal: flex items-center gap-2 with flex-1 for non-last items
- Vertical: flex flex-col gap-2

StepperTrigger: Clickable circle indicator
- Props: React.ComponentProps button
- data-slot=stepper-trigger
- Shows step number (step + 1) by default; shows checkmark icon when completed
- Calls onStepChange(step) on click (respects linear constraint and disabled state)
- aria-current=step when active
- Circle styling: inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-full size-8 shrink-0 border-2 text-sm font-medium transition-colors
- Uses IconPlaceholder for the checkmark icon (base version)

StepperTitle: Step title text
- Props: React.ComponentProps div
- data-slot=stepper-title, cn-stepper-title cn-font-heading text-sm font-medium
- Text color varies by state via parent data-state + group selectors

StepperDescription: Step description text
- Props: React.ComponentProps div
- data-slot=stepper-description, cn-stepper-description text-xs text-muted-foreground

StepperSeparator: Connector line between steps
- Props: React.ComponentProps div
- Placed inside a StepperItem, reads useStepperItem() for completed state
- data-slot=stepper-separator, data-state=completed/inactive
- Horizontal: h-0.5 flex-1; Vertical: w-0.5 ml-4 min-h-4
- Color: cn-stepper-separator token; completed=primary, inactive=border

StepperContent: Content panel for active step
- Props: React.ComponentProps div plus step: number
- Placed outside StepperItem at Stepper root level; uses useStepper() to check step === activeStep
- Only renders children when active
- data-slot=stepper-content

### Exports

Stepper, StepperItem, StepperTrigger, StepperTitle, StepperDescription, StepperSeparator, StepperContent, useStepper

---

## Step 2: Styled Version (base-nova)

File: apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/stepper.tsx

Same structure as Step 1 but:
- Imports from @/lib/utils (not @/registry/bases/base/lib/utils)
- Imports CheckIcon from lucide-react directly (not IconPlaceholder)
- Full Tailwind utility classes instead of cn- semantic tokens

Key styling:
- Trigger active: border-primary bg-primary text-primary-foreground
- Trigger completed: border-primary bg-primary text-primary-foreground
- Trigger inactive: border-muted-foreground/25 bg-muted text-muted-foreground
- Separator completed: bg-primary
- Separator inactive: bg-border
- Title active/completed: text-foreground
- Title inactive: text-muted-foreground

---

## Step 3: Demo Example

File: apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx

Follows the pattern from tabs-example.tsx: use client, default export, ExampleWrapper + individual Example sub-components.

### Variations

1. StepperBasic: Horizontal 3-step with titles only
2. StepperWithDescriptions: Horizontal with titles + descriptions
3. StepperVertical: Vertical orientation
4. StepperWithContent: Full wizard with content panels + Next/Previous buttons (uses React.useState)
5. StepperLinear: linear=true, can only go forward
6. StepperControlled: Controlled state with external Next/Previous/Reset buttons

### Imports

- Example, ExampleWrapper from @/registry/bases/base/components/example
- Button from @/registry/bases/base/ui/button
- All Stepper sub-components from @/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper

---

## Step 4: Registry Entries

### 4a. Append to apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/_registry.ts

Add stepper entry with name stepper, type registry:ui, files pointing to ui/stepper.tsx, and meta.links for docs and examples URLs.

### 4b. Append to apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts

Add stepper-example entry with name stepper-example, title Stepper, type registry:example, registryDependencies [stepper, button, example], files pointing to examples/stepper-example.tsx.

---

## Key Design Decisions

1. Numeric 0-indexed steps (not string values like Tabs): steppers have inherent ordering; step < activeStep = completed
2. Explicit step prop on StepperItem: simpler and more predictable than auto-indexing from render order
3. StepperSeparator inside StepperItem: can read useStepperItem() context to determine completed state
4. StepperContent outside StepperItem: takes its own step prop, reads useStepper(), renders at root level below the step indicators
5. linear prop: when true, only completed steps and the next step are clickable
6. Controlled/uncontrolled: defaultStep for uncontrolled, step+onStepChange for controlled

## Reference Files (patterns to follow)

- apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/carousel.tsx: React context pattern, cn- tokens, IconPlaceholder
- apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/carousel.tsx: Styled version, lucide icons, @/lib/utils
- apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/card.tsx: Layout compound pattern with cn- tokens
- apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/card.tsx: Styled layout compound
- apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/tabs.tsx: Orientation handling with data-orientation + group selectors
- apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/tabs-example.tsx: Example file structure

---

## Verification

1. TypeScript: Run pnpm tsc --noEmit in apps/v4/ to verify no type errors
2. Registry parse: The registry.ts runs z.array(registryItemSchema).parse() at import time, if the entry is malformed it will throw
3. Visual: Run pnpm dev in apps/v4/ and navigate to the stepper example page to verify all 6 demo variations render correctly in both horizontal and vertical orientations
4. Build: Run pnpm build to ensure the build pipeline picks up the new registry entries
